name: present_ticker_normal_path
description: Ticker 'XYZ' is present in all 50 historical days with amplitude 0.002 (smaller than SPY's 0.005). The normal branch fires for every day: returns_matrix uses XYZ's own daily_ret, not the SPY fallback. Historical XYZ returns are +/-0.002 -> +/-0.2 pct after scaling. current_symphony_return=0.3 (last_percent_change=0.003) is above all XYZ returns (max 0.2) -> below_count=simulation_paths -> prob=0.0. Negative control for the missing-ticker fixture: result (0.0) differs from the spy-substitution result because the own-returns distribution has a smaller range than SPY. num_days is 50 so the history is eligible-sufficient under Ruling 2 (AC-4).
derivation: XYZ present all days -> returns_matrix uses XYZ daily_ret (+-0.002 -> +-0.2 pct). current_symphony_return=0.3 > max(0.2) -> all sim paths are below current_return -> below_count=simulation_paths -> prob=0.0 (a deterministic boundary result, not a random draw). Differs from missing-ticker because substituting SPY (+-0.5) vs using XYZ (+-0.2) changes the return distribution. Re-captured from the fixed producer at branch fix/mc-knn-exit-gate HEAD c3834d0 (Cluster 2), num_days raised 30->50 so the history is eligible-sufficient under Ruling 2. Provenance: captured-from-the-fixed-producer.
